MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-25-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-25-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-25-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IGAL KOIMAN'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO SOLANO COUNTY. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY ALL TRANSFER FEES. THE GENERAL RULE UNDER CCP 395 IS THAT VENUE IS IN THE COUNTY OF DEFENDANT'S RESIDENCE. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CITE AN APPLICABLE EXCEPTION TO THE GENERAL RULE. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT VENUE COULD BE PROPER BASED ON A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM IS IRRELEVANT. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES NO SUCH CLAIM. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $1,000 IN FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CCP 396B(B) DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S UNWILLINGNESS TO STIPULATE TO THE TRANSFER OF THIS ACTION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA REDING, CSR 	711, EMAIL: LREDING@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IGAL KOIMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA REDING, CSR 	711, EMAIL: LREDING@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Aug-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Alexander Perez of Treyzon & Associates LLP (310-407-7888) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kristin N. Blake of Klinedinst PC (916-444-7573) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party, Igal Koiman

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court modifies and confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT IGAL KOIMAN'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is granted. The case is transferred to Solano County. Plaintiff shall pay all transfer fees. The general rule under CCP 395 is that venue is in the county of defendant's residence. Plaintiff does not cite an applicable exception to the general rule. Plaintiff's argument that venue could be proper based on a breach of contract claim is irrelevant. The complaint alleges no such claim. Defendant is awarded $1,000 in fees and costs pursuant to CCP 396b(b) due to plaintiff's unwillingness to stipulate to the transfer of this action. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court modifies and confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT IGAL KOIMAN'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint is off calendar in light of the ruling on the motion to change venue.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)
